rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE EMILIA DI FRANCO GLESSI TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Anthony Muschi's Motion for Summary Judgment is denied. If Petitioners were to succeed in invalidating the trust, the property would pass to the decedent's estate. Petitioners (who are intestate heirs as well as beneficiaries under the 1988 Will) could then invalidate the 1995-2011 testamentary documents, giving them standing under the 1988 Will. Construing the evidence in the light most favorable to the non-moving party, the Court finds triable issues of material fact regarding the Petitioners' Third Amended Petition. The prevailing party shall prepare a proposed order. Any party choosing to contest the tentative must notify the court and opposing counsel no later than 9/15/2015, at 4:00 p.m.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE EMILIA DI FRANCO GLESSI TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioners Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Petitioners' Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings is denied. Muschi filed an answer to the Third Amended Petition on June 29, 2015, the same day Petitioners filed their motion. The answer moots the motion for judgment on the pleadings. The prevailing party shall prepare a proposed order. Any party choosing to contest the tentative ruling must notify the Court and opposing counsel no later than 9/15/2015, at 4:00 p.m.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE EMILIA DI FRANCO GLESSI TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioners Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Anthony Muschis Answer And Enter Default Against Respondent Anthony Muschi Individually And As Trustee Of The Emilia Di Franco Glessi 2012 Revocable Living Trust Dated November 13, 2012 And To Set A Default Prove Up Hearing
rulings.Rulings: The motion to strike Muschi's answer and enter default is denied. CCP Section 585(b) provides for entry of default only where the defendant or respondent made no response or appearance in the case. Here, the respondent responded via a motion for judgment ont he pleadings and then filed an answer to the Third Amended Petition. Based on the fact that Muschi has repeatedly appeared in this action and filed responsive pleadings to the Third Amended Petition, the Court denies Petitioners' request to strike Muschi's answer and order the clerk to enter default. The prevailing party shall submit a proposed order. Any party choosing to contest the tentative ruling must notify the Court and opposing counsel no later than September 15, 2015, at 4:00 p.m.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EMILA DI FRANCO GLESSI
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Anthony Muschi's Motion for Summary Judgment is denied. Under Swift v. Superior Court (1952) 39 Cal.2d 358, the Court can and does treat Muschi's motion for summary judgment as a written objection to probate of the 1988 Will and thus a will contest as contemplated by Sections 8250-8254 of the Probate Code. Muschi's motion is denied because triable issues of material fact exist with respect to the validity of the 1995 will and whether it effectively revokes the 1988 Will. Petitioner Probert's request for monetary sanctions is denied. The prevailing party shall prepare a proposed order. Any party choosing to contest the tentative ruling shall notify the Court and opposing counsel no later than 9/15/2015, at 4:00 p.m.
